results.agent_output:
Updated [src/vector_add.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_076_amp-deep3_on9ak9gf/src/vector_add.cu#L20-L43) so the kernel only writes when `i < N`, and the launch now rounds the grid size up with `(N + BLOCK_SIZE - 1) / BLOCK_SIZE` instead of truncating to full blocks only. That fixes both the missing tail elements and the potential out-of-bounds access in the final partial block.

Verified with `python3 -m pytest tests/test_vector_add.py -q` (`7 passed`), and with direct `nvcc` runs for `--size 1000`, `1025`, and `257`, all of which now report `MISMATCHES=0` and `MATCH=1`.

results.error: fail_to_pass tests already pass before agent ran (bad task)

results.agent_output:
Updated the GPU histogram kernel in [src/histogram.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_078_amp-deep3_8fmi5o00/src/histogram.cu#L22-L27) to use `atomicAdd(&hist[bin], 1)` instead of a plain `hist[bin] += 1`. That fixes the race condition where multiple threads could overwrite each other’s increments and lose counts.

I verified the fix with a local `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` build and ran the larger failing cases from the report. The runs for `N=100000`, `N=50000`, and `N=20000` all returned `MISMATCHES=0`, `CPU_TOTAL == GPU_TOTAL`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the exclusive scan bug by correcting the Blelloch down-sweep initialization: the shared-memory root is now reset to the identity element `0.0f` before the down-sweep begins. That was the missing step causing the scan to behave like a shifted/inclusive variant instead of a true exclusive scan.

Verified with a local `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` build in the workspace and ran the binary for sizes `16`, `128`, and `256`. All three runs reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `FIRST_ELEMENT=0.0000`, `FIRST_OK=1`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the boundary bug in [src/conv1d.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_080_amp-deep3_1zvqp3hs/src/conv1d.cu#L29-L62). The left shared-memory halo was being loaded with `gid - radius + lid`, which effectively added `threadIdx.x` twice and misaligned the halo for boundary tiles. I changed it to use the block start explicitly, `block_start - radius + lid`, and kept out-of-range accesses zero-padded with a full bounds check.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running the same kinds of cases the bug report calls out. The results were clean: `MATCH=1`, `MISMATCHES=0`, and `BOUNDARY_ERRORS=0` for `--size 500 --ksize 5 --seed 42`, `--size 300 --ksize 11 --seed 99`, `--size 200 --ksize 7 --seed 13`, and `--size 128 --ksize 3 --seed 1`.

Fixed the reduction bug in [src/reduction.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_081_amp-deep3_h25ero46/src/reduction.cu#L21-L56).

The wrong sums came from two problems in the final warp phase:
1. The shuffle mask only enabled the lower 16 lanes, so half the warp never participated.
2. The first warp started shuffling from `sdata[tid]` alone, which dropped the remaining partial sums in `sdata[32..63]`.

I changed the warp helper to use the full warp mask at [lines 21-25](file:///tmp/bench_task_081_amp-deep3_h25ero46/src/reduction.cu#L21-L25), and I changed the final warp step to begin from `sdata[tid] + sdata[tid + WARP_SIZE]` at [lines 53-56](file:///tmp/bench_task_081_amp-deep3_h25ero46/src/reduction.cu#L53-L56).

Verification:
- Compiled with `nvcc src/reduction.cu -o reduction_test -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`
- Ran `./reduction_test` for sizes `512`, `7777`, `10000`, and `100000`
- All runs reported `MATCH=1` with relative error around `1e-9` to `1e-8`

Fixed the CSR kernel in [src/spmv_csr.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_082_amp-deep3_l2_6cvd2/src/spmv_csr.cu#L25-L40). The bug was exactly in the row bounds and accumulator setup: [the kernel now initializes `sum` to `0.0f`](file:///tmp/bench_task_082_amp-deep3_l2_6cvd2/src/spmv_csr.cu#L31) so empty rows return zero, and [it uses `row_ptr[row + 1]` for `end` on every row](file:///tmp/bench_task_082_amp-deep3_l2_6cvd2/src/spmv_csr.cu#L33-L38), which makes the last row follow standard CSR semantics instead of the broken `nnz - 1` special case.

Verification passed with `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_spmv_csr.py` (`7 passed`) and with a direct CUDA build/run using `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`, which reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `EMPTY_ROW_ERRORS=0`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the CUDA bitonic sort so non-power-of-2 inputs sort correctly.

The change does two things in the implementation:
- It pads the full device buffer range instead of launching too few threads for the padding kernel, which was leaving part of the padded tail uninitialized and letting non-`FLT_MAX` garbage leak into the first `N` results.
- It switches the compare/swap stage to the standard bitonic network form, using `partner = i ^ j` and `ascending = (i & k) == 0`, so the merge direction is determined from the correct stage bit.

Verification:
- `pytest` is not installed in this workspace, so I verified by compiling directly with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`.
- The compiled binary now reports `MATCH=1` and `SORTED=1` for `--size 100`, `300`, `1000`, and the power-of-2 control case `256`.

I also left the verification binary `bitonic_sort_check` in the current directory after the build.

Fixed the stream compaction bug in [src/stream_compact.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_wasi4x5l/src/stream_compact.cu#L34-L105) by changing the scan used for scatter positions from inclusive to exclusive. The scatter kernel now writes selected elements to the correct zero-based output slot, and the total count is derived as `last_exclusive_scan + last_flag`, which keeps the GPU count aligned with the CPU reference.

The key change is in the new [exclusive scan kernel and launch path](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_wasi4x5l/src/stream_compact.cu#L34-L52) plus the [count recovery logic](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_wasi4x5l/src/stream_compact.cu#L89-L105). I also added a small `N <= 0` guard in [gpu_compact](file:///tmp/bench_task_084_amp-deep3_wasi4x5l/src/stream_compact.cu#L68-L74) to avoid invalid launches on empty inputs.

Verified by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running:
- default case: `CPU_COUNT=94`, `GPU_COUNT=94`, `MISMATCHES=0`, `MATCH=1`
- `--size 64 --threshold 25 --seed 7`: `MATCH=1`
- `--size 256 --threshold 90 --seed 11`: `MATCH=1`

Implemented the signed-integer fix in [src/radix_sort.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_085_amp-deep3_jmotgwnp/src/radix_sort.cu#L25-L31) and wired it into the GPU sort flow in [gpu_radix_sort](file:///tmp/bench_task_085_amp-deep3_jmotgwnp/src/radix_sort.cu#L84-L122). The change adds a small CUDA kernel that XORs each value with `0x80000000u` before the radix passes so signed values sort in the correct order, then XORs them again afterward to restore the original integers.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running mixed-sign and positive-only cases. The checked runs all reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `SORTED=1`, `NEG_AFTER_POS=0`, and `MATCH=1` for seeds `42`, `99`, `13`, and positive-only mode.

results.agent_output:
Patched [src/cg_reduce.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_087_amp-deep3_x95eedro/src/cg_reduce.cu#L22-L102) so the reduction no longer assumes warp-sized tiles. The tile-local step now uses `TILE_SZ` and the cooperative-groups tile API (`thread_rank()`, `meta_group_rank()`, `meta_group_size()`), and the cross-tile aggregation is a shared-memory tree reduction over all tile leaders instead of a fixed 32-thread warp reduction. I also added kernel dispatch for tile sizes `1` and `2` alongside the existing `4/8/16/32` cases.

Verification passed by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running the binary for tile sizes `1`, `2`, `4`, `8`, `16`, and `32`; each case reported `MATCH=1` with relative error around `1e-8`. `pytest` itself wasn’t installed in this environment, so I verified directly through `nvcc` and the program output instead.

Fixed the unified-memory ping-pong bug in [src/jacobi_um.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_088_amp-deep3_k99i1zim/src/jacobi_um.cu#L52-L103). The key change is in [the Jacobi UM driver](file:///tmp/bench_task_088_amp-deep3_k99i1zim/src/jacobi_um.cu#L71-L99): it now prefetches both buffers intentionally, launches the kernel with `x0` as the read buffer and `x1` as the write buffer, then swaps them so the freshly computed iterate becomes the next input. The final result is still prefetched back to CPU before copying out.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm` and running representative cases. The previously failing configurations I checked all now report `MISMATCHES=0` and `MATCH=1`, including `--size 64 --iters 20 --seed 42`, `--size 100 --iters 10 --seed 13`, and `--size 48 --iters 30 --seed 777`.

results.agent_output:
I fixed the warp-divergence bug in the CUDA kernel by making the warp intrinsics use the real active-lane mask and by correcting the reduction itself.

The change does three things:
- Uses `__activemask()` as the sync mask, then ballots with that mask instead of the broken lower-16-bit mask.
- Reduces a running per-lane contribution with `__shfl_down_sync(active_mask, ...)` instead of repeatedly shuffling the original value, which was producing wrong sums for mixed predicates.
- Broadcasts the final sums with the same active mask, so only live lanes in the warp contribute, including partial tail warps.

Verification passed:
- `python3 -m pytest -q` → `5 passed`
- direct compiled run → `MISMATCHES=0`, `MATCH=1`

Fixed in [src/wmma_gemm.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_091_amp-deep3_i3avq0sp/src/wmma_gemm.cu#L31-L108).

The WMMA kernel now matches the actual row-major storage for both inputs, and matrix A’s fragment load uses the correct leading dimension. I also passed explicit `lda`/`ldb`/`ldc` strides into the kernel and padded A, B, and C to 16-wide WMMA tile sizes in [gpu_gemm](file:///tmp/bench_task_091_amp-deep3_i3avq0sp/src/wmma_gemm.cu#L62-L108), so the implementation is correct for non-multiple `M`, `N`, and `K` as well instead of only exact 16x16 tiles.

Verification:
- `nvcc src/wmma_gemm.cu -o wmma_gemm -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm -arch=sm_70`
- `./wmma_gemm --M 16 --N 16 --K 16 --seed 13` -> `MATCH=1`
- `./wmma_gemm --M 64 --N 64 --K 32 --seed 99` -> `MATCH=1`
- `./wmma_gemm --M 17 --N 19 --K 21 --seed 7` -> `MATCH=1`

I left the workspace-local `wmma_gemm` binary in the current directory from verification.

fail_to_pass tests already pass before agent ran (bad task)

results.agent_output:
Updated [src/dp_mergesort.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_h_06gma9/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L6-L100) to remove the device-side deadlock path and stop the recursive launch explosion. The recursive sorter now launches its two child sorts with `cudaStreamFireAndForget` and schedules the merge in a separate `merge_kernel` on `cudaStreamTailLaunch` ([lines 55-79](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_h_06gma9/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L55-L79)), so each merge runs only after its own child subtrees finish without calling device-side `cudaDeviceSynchronize()`. I also added a practical base-case cutoff, `INSERTION_SORT_THRESHOLD = 32`, so it uses insertion sort for small ranges instead of launching one kernel per element ([lines 6-7](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_h_06gma9/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L6-L7), [67-69](file:///tmp/bench_task_094_amp-deep3_h_06gma9/src/dp_mergesort.cu#L67-L69)).

Verification succeeded with the requested build command, `nvcc src/dp_mergesort.cu -o dp_mergesort_test -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`, and runs at sizes `1`, `33`, `64`, `512`, `1024`, and `4096` all reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `SORTED=1`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the shared-memory ping-pong logic in [src/tiled_gemm.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_095_amp-deep3_n9ttbs4k/src/tiled_gemm.cu#L38-L66). The kernel now keeps a stable `read_buf` for the tile being consumed, prefetches the next tile into the opposite `write_buf`, and only swaps buffers after the iteration barrier. That prevents the next load from overwriting the tile still being used in the MAC loop.

I verified it by compiling with `nvcc` and running the same non-trivial sizes covered by the existing tests: `64x64x64`, `128x128x64`, `32x64x128`, `64x32x32`, and `256x256x256`. All now report `MISMATCHES=0` and `MATCH=1`, with only small floating-point error on the order of `1e-6`. `pytest` itself isn’t installed in this workspace, so I validated through the compiled binary directly.

Fixed the CUDA BFS race in [src/bfs_warp.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_096_amp-deep3_pznia1t3/src/bfs_warp.cu#L39-L65) by making discovery single-winner with `atomicCAS(dist + u, -1, next_level)` and making frontier reservation unique with `atomicAdd(out_size, 1)`. I also changed the warp neighbor loop to stride over absolute CSR edge indices, so each warp walks its own adjacency segment directly.

While verifying, I hit a separate memory corruption bug in the local graph generator, so I repaired [generate_graph` in src/bfs_warp.cu`](file:///tmp/bench_task_096_amp-deep3_pznia1t3/src/bfs_warp.cu#L122-L156) to build `row_ptr` and `col_idx` from a consistent CSR count. Without that fix, some seeds crashed before the CUDA code even ran.

Verified with a local workspace build using `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`, then ran:
- `./bfs_warp_test --size 500 --seed 42`
- `./bfs_warp_test --size 1000 --seed 99`
- `./bfs_warp_test --size 300 --seed 13`
- `./bfs_warp_test --size 200 --degree 8 --seed 777`
- `./bfs_warp_test --size 500 --degree 2 --seed 42`

All of them reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `CPU_UNVISITED=0`, `GPU_UNVISITED=0`, and `MATCH=1`.

Fixed the CUDA FFT bugs in [src/fft_ct.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_v5rt7hb_/src/fft_ct.cu#L45-L145).

The key changes are in the [bit-reversal kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_v5rt7hb_/src/fft_ct.cu#L45-L60), which now reverses the full `log2(N)` bits, and the [butterfly kernel / stage loop](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_v5rt7hb_/src/fft_ct.cu#L65-L124), which now uses an explicit full-span/half-span layout so the partner index is `i + span/2` and the twiddle uses the correct forward/inverse sign convention. I also corrected the [inverse scaling](file:///tmp/bench_task_097_amp-deep3_v5rt7hb_/src/fft_ct.cu#L127-L138) from `2/N` to `1/N`.

Verification passed with `nvcc -rdc=true src/fft_ct.cu -lcudadevrt -lm -o fft_ct`, then:
- `./fft_ct --size 256` → `MISMATCHES=0`, `MAX_ERROR=1.151820e-05`
- `./fft_ct --size 256 --inverse` → `MISMATCHES=0`, `MAX_ERROR=4.499298e-08`

I also swept multiple power-of-two sizes up to `512` in both forward and inverse modes, and all runs matched the CPU reference.

results.agent_output:
Fixed the GPU path in [src/ray_aabb.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_099_amp-deep3_zqx82xbm/src/ray_aabb.cu#L83-L146) so it now matches the CPU reference semantics. The key changes were: initializing the slab interval with `tmax = FLT_MAX`, explicitly swapping `t1`/`t2` when a direction component is negative, initializing `best_t` to `FLT_MAX`, popping the traversal stack with `--sp` instead of `sp--`, and tightening the leaf check to `is_leaf == 1`.

Verification passed with the requested CUDA build and several runtime sweeps:
- `nvcc -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm src/ray_aabb.cu -o ray_aabb`
- `./ray_aabb` → `MISMATCHES=0`, `MATCH=1`
- `./ray_aabb --seed 1 --prims 16 --rays 512` → `MISMATCHES=0`
- `./ray_aabb --seed 123 --prims 32 --rays 1024` → `MISMATCHES=0`
- `./ray_aabb --seed 999 --prims 8 --rays 128` → `MISMATCHES=0`

Fixed the CUDA force path in [src/md_neighbor.cu](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_g1jhgydn/src/md_neighbor.cu#L51-L155).

The GPU neighbor list now uses `r² < cutoff²`, skips self-pairs, and only stores each pair once with `j > i` in [build_neighbor_list](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_g1jhgydn/src/md_neighbor.cu#L51-L74). The force kernel now matches the CPU reference by computing the full Lennard-Jones term with `sigma_r12 = sigma_r6 * sigma_r6`, forming the force vector from that scalar and `dr`, and applying equal-and-opposite updates with atomics so Newton’s third law is preserved in [lj_force_kernel](file:///tmp/bench_task_100_amp-deep3_g1jhgydn/src/md_neighbor.cu#L79-L123).

Verification:
- Compiled successfully with `nvcc src/md_neighbor.cu -o md_neighbor -rdc=true -lcudadevrt -lm`
- Ran `./md_neighbor` for `--size 16`, `32`, `64`, `128`, `256`, plus `--cutoff 4.0`
- All checked runs reported `MISMATCHES=0`, `MATCH=1`, and `NAN_COUNT=0`
- `GPU_MOMENTUM` stayed well below the test threshold in the momentum case

I couldn’t run `pytest` directly because `pytest` isn’t installed in this workspace, so I validated with the compiled binary against the same scenarios the test file covers.
